meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve No Portfolio (Beginner)
content.seo_description: Learn how to generate passive income and overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you stop waiting for permission and start shipping. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated refinement, you can deploy a functional, revenue-generating micro-SaaS in days, transforming your GitHub from a ghost town into a showcase of real-world technical competency.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the weight of a dozen 'Coming Soon' repos hanging over your career prospects. The industry demands experience, but you cannot get experience without a job, and you cannot get a job without a portfolio. It is the classic paradox that stalls thousands of brilliant engineering students every year.

But what if you bypassed the gatekeepers entirely? By building a micro-SaaS designed for passive income, you aren't just writing code—you are building a business asset. This approach shifts the narrative from 'student with no experience' to 'founder who has shipped.' It is time to turn your screen from a source of anxiety into a machine for growth.

steps.title: Define Your Micro-SaaS Niche
steps.description: Identify a specific, painful problem for a niche audience. Focus on a tool that automates one task, like a content repurposing script or a data scraper, to ensure a quick MVP launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product architect. Suggest 3 high-demand, low-complexity micro-SaaS ideas that solve a specific problem for freelance creators, requiring less than 48 hours of development time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review these 3 product ideas and rank them by ease of monetization and technical complexity.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the foundational boilerplate. This ensures industry-standard structure, clean separation of concerns, and immediate testability for your project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular Next.js 14 stack with Tailwind CSS, Supabase integration, and Stripe checkout flow. Use TypeScript and include a clear folder structure for API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure based on the Antigravity modular template.'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your business logic. By iterating with an AI pair programmer, you learn how to handle edge cases and data validation effectively.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure server-side function to handle user authentication and Stripe subscription status verification. Ensure errors are logged to a mock dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Implement the authentication middleware and Stripe webhook handler in the src/lib directory.'

steps.title: Automated Testing and Refinement
steps.description: A portfolio is only as good as its reliability. Use Claude Code to generate unit tests that prove your code works under pressure, a key skill for senior developers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite using Vitest for the user registration and payment flow. Include a test case for invalid card scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run the generated unit tests and fix any regression errors found in the auth flow.'

steps.title: Deployment and Portfolio Packaging
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and document your 'Why'. A great portfolio piece isn't just the code; it's the story of how you solved the user's problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md that highlights the problem, the technical stack, the challenges faced, and the metrics of success for this micro-SaaS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a high-conversion README.md based on the project files and push to GitHub.'

pro_tips: Focus on 'Read-only' revenue: Build tools that consume data rather than manage it to minimize database complexity.
Document the 'Why' over the 'How': Future employers care more about your architectural decisions than the syntax you used.
Ship the 'ugly' version first: 100% of a bad design is better than 0% of a perfect one for your portfolio.
Use GitHub Issues to track your own bugs: It makes your repo look like a professional, managed engineering project.
Add a 'Built by [Your Name]' footer with a link to your LinkedIn to turn every user into a potential networking lead.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, share it on IndieHackers and Twitter/X. Collect user feedback, document the iteration process in your README, and link the project prominently in your CV.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student waiting for a break; you are a builder who has proven they can ship. Stop collecting tutorials and start collecting users. The path to a senior-level career starts with one deployed project. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
